Very long article

Members of the lifeboat crew received commendations for their work during the height of the storm.

Hotel owners said tourists were beginning to return, with bookings recovering after a brief slump during the storm.

Local schools reopened on Tuesday, with most pupils returning despite some bus services being temporarily diverted.

Local schools reopened on Tuesday, with most pupils returning despite some bus services being temporarily diverted.

Researchers from the regional university surveyed the cliffs, citing concerns about ongoing erosion accelerated by recent weather.

Power had been fully restored to all neighborhoods by Tuesday evening, although officials warned of intermittent outages.

Community organizers said the local festival, scheduled for the following weekend, would proceed as planned.

The seaside town of Aldermouth has been celebrating the reopening of its harbor following a turbulent week of storms.

Hotel owners said tourists were beginning to return, with bookings recovering after a brief slump during the storm.

Traffic on the coastal road was reduced to a single lane until further notice as crews finished clearing fallen branches.

Community organizers said the local festival, scheduled for the following weekend, would proceed as planned.

Residents praised the swift response of emergency services and volunteer groups who began clearing debris before dawn.

Hotel owners said tourists were beginning to return, with bookings recovering after a brief slump during the storm.

The local fishing fleet returned to sea on Wednesday morning, with smaller boats reporting their first catches by lunchtime.

Community organizers said the local festival, scheduled for the following weekend, would proceed as planned.

The coastguard issued a reminder that conditions remained changeable and advised caution for casual swimmers and surfers.

The chamber of commerce confirmed that nearly all small businesses had reopened within forty-eight hours of the all-clear.

Local schools reopened on Tuesday, with most pupils returning despite some bus services being temporarily diverted.

Power had been fully restored to all neighborhoods by Tuesday evening, although officials warned of intermittent outages.

Traffic on the coastal road was reduced to a single lane until further notice as crews finished clearing fallen branches.

Community organizers said the local festival, scheduled for the following weekend, would proceed as planned.

The local fishing fleet returned to sea on Wednesday morning, with smaller boats reporting their first catches by lunchtime.

Hotel owners said tourists were beginning to return, with bookings recovering after a brief slump during the storm.

Local schools reopened on Tuesday, with most pupils returning despite some bus services being temporarily diverted.

Officials said inspection of the seawall would take several more days as engineers assessed the extent of the damage.

Volunteers handed out hot drinks and packed lunches to clean-up crews at the harborside community center.

The mayor said an emergency budget would cover the immediate repair costs, with state assistance still being negotiated.

Power had been fully restored to all neighborhoods by Tuesday evening, although officials warned of intermittent outages.

The coastguard issued a reminder that conditions remained changeable and advised caution for casual swimmers and surfers.

Residents praised the swift response of emergency services and volunteer groups who began clearing debris before dawn.

The seaside town of Aldermouth has been celebrating the reopening of its harbor following a turbulent week of storms.

The seaside town of Aldermouth has been celebrating the reopening of its harbor following a turbulent week of storms.

Residents praised the swift response of emergency services and volunteer groups who began clearing debris before dawn.

Local schools reopened on Tuesday, with most pupils returning despite some bus services being temporarily diverted.

Researchers from the regional university surveyed the cliffs, citing concerns about ongoing erosion accelerated by recent weather.

Community organizers said the local festival, scheduled for the following weekend, would proceed as planned.

Traffic on the coastal road was reduced to a single lane until further notice as crews finished clearing fallen branches.

The seaside town of Aldermouth has been celebrating the reopening of its harbor following a turbulent week of storms.

The chamber of commerce confirmed that nearly all small businesses had reopened within forty-eight hours of the all-clear.

The local fishing fleet returned to sea on Wednesday morning, with smaller boats reporting their first catches by lunchtime.

Volunteers handed out hot drinks and packed lunches to clean-up crews at the harborside community center.

The chamber of commerce confirmed that nearly all small businesses had reopened within forty-eight hours of the all-clear.

Residents praised the swift response of emergency services and volunteer groups who began clearing debris before dawn.

Hotel owners said tourists were beginning to return, with bookings recovering after a brief slump during the storm.

Traffic on the coastal road was reduced to a single lane until further notice as crews finished clearing fallen branches.

Researchers from the regional university surveyed the cliffs, citing concerns about ongoing erosion accelerated by recent weather.

Hotel owners said tourists were beginning to return, with bookings recovering after a brief slump during the storm.

Officials said inspection of the seawall would take several more days as engineers assessed the extent of the damage.

Members of the lifeboat crew received commendations for their work during the height of the storm.

Local schools reopened on Tuesday, with most pupils returning despite some bus services being temporarily diverted.

Officials said inspection of the seawall would take several more days as engineers assessed the extent of the damage.

Traffic on the coastal road was reduced to a single lane until further notice as crews finished clearing fallen branches.

Members of the lifeboat crew received commendations for their work during the height of the storm.

The coastguard issued a reminder that conditions remained changeable and advised caution for casual swimmers and surfers.

The mayor said an emergency budget would cover the immediate repair costs, with state assistance still being negotiated.

Community organizers said the local festival, scheduled for the following weekend, would proceed as planned.

The seaside town of Aldermouth has been celebrating the reopening of its harbor following a turbulent week of storms.

The coastguard issued a reminder that conditions remained changeable and advised caution for casual swimmers and surfers.

Power had been fully restored to all neighborhoods by Tuesday evening, although officials warned of intermittent outages.

Officials said inspection of the seawall would take several more days as engineers assessed the extent of the damage.

Hotel owners said tourists were beginning to return, with bookings recovering after a brief slump during the storm.

The chamber of commerce confirmed that nearly all small businesses had reopened within forty-eight hours of the all-clear.

The local fishing fleet returned to sea on Wednesday morning, with smaller boats reporting their first catches by lunchtime.

The coastguard issued a reminder that conditions remained changeable and advised caution for casual swimmers and surfers.

The seaside town of Aldermouth has been celebrating the reopening of its harbor following a turbulent week of storms.

Community organizers said the local festival, scheduled for the following weekend, would proceed as planned.

The coastguard issued a reminder that conditions remained changeable and advised caution for casual swimmers and surfers.

Community organizers said the local festival, scheduled for the following weekend, would proceed as planned.

Power had been fully restored to all neighborhoods by Tuesday evening, although officials warned of intermittent outages.

The seaside town of Aldermouth has been celebrating the reopening of its harbor following a turbulent week of storms.

Volunteers handed out hot drinks and packed lunches to clean-up crews at the harborside community center.

The local fishing fleet returned to sea on Wednesday morning, with smaller boats reporting their first catches by lunchtime.

Power had been fully restored to all neighborhoods by Tuesday evening, although officials warned of intermittent outages.
